blockers.description: In-game visual confirmation pending: user has not yet reported whether downstream Thread Manager windows show non-zero split values after the connection_out_set fix
blockers.workaround: Diagnostic logs confirm transfer=2 and wdata populated; distribution math path for STM_STORAGE (equal split) is correct. Likely working but unconfirmed visually.

human_actions_pending.action: Launch game with current installed ZIPs. Place STM with 2 downstream Thread Manager windows. Report: do downstream windows show ~2.6GHz each (or any non-zero split)?
human_actions_pending.context: Last diagnostic log showed transfer=2, wdata populated, demands={0.0, 0.0}. Thread Manager windows classify as STM_STORAGE (role=2) -- they get equal-split overflow via _set_storages(). Visual confirmation is the last unknown before Plan 02-03.

decisions.decision: Use connection_out_set signal + tick-time fallback to call update_connections()
decisions.rationale: game engine's _on_create_connection() was not reaching our GDScript override; BetterSplitters reference mod uses connection_out_set directly

decisions.decision: Thread Manager downstream windows classify as STM_STORAGE (not STM_CONSUMER)
decisions.rationale: clock_speed is FLOW type (type=1), not MATERIAL. STM_CONSUMER requires material inputs + goal property. Thread Managers have dependent=[] so equal-split via _set_storages() is correct for ratio mode.

decisions.decision: Use .NET ZipArchive with forward-slash path replacement for packaging
decisions.rationale: Compress-Archive on Windows produces backslash paths; Godot mod loader requires forward-slash paths in ZIP entries

next_action: Ask user: did the downstream Thread Manager windows show non-zero split (~2.6GHz each) after the fix? Then proceed with Plan 02-03: remove diagnostics, add compat shims, repackage, full 14-point verification.
context_notes: The connection_out_set fix is the core breakthrough. transfer went from 0 to 2. wdata populates correctly. demands=0 is expected (STM_STORAGE windows have no goal, so demand=0 and equal-split applies). The only open question is whether the game UI reflects the split visually. Once confirmed, Plan 02-03 is straightforward: strip prints, add has()/get() guards per the plan spec, repackage, verify.
